reasoning: Output B provides a comprehensive, highly structured execution plan with detailed phase breakdowns, specific tool calls (TeamCreate, Agent with team_name, SendMessage), explicit resume logic (Story 1.2 starts at Step 3), escalation ladders, sub-agent lifecycle management, and a complete flow estimate. Output A, while it correctly identifies that Story 1.1 should be skipped and Story 1.2 should start at development, explicitly admits it would NOT call TeamCreate, would NOT use team-based sub-agents, would NOT invoke BMAD slash commands, and would lack the collaborative escalation pattern. Output A reads more as a self-assessment of limitations than an actionable execution plan. Output B is a fully actionable orchestration plan; Output A is a thoughtful but self-admittedly incomplete plan.

strengths: Correctly identifies Story 1.1 should be skipped
Correctly reasons that ready-for-dev means start at development
weaknesses: Explicitly states it would NOT call TeamCreate
Would not use team-based sub-agents with team_name parameter
Would not invoke BMAD-specific skills (bmad-bmm-create-story, bmad-bmm-dev-story, etc.)
Reads as a limitations document rather than an execution plan

strengths: Complete phase-by-phase execution plan with 7 phases
Correct resume logic: Story 1.1 skipped, Story 1.2 starts at Step 3
Agent tool used with team_name parameter throughout
Sub-agent lifecycle properly managed with shutdown_request
Tools summary table and sub-agent skills table provided
weaknesses: Minor: very long document, could be slightly more concise

details.text: Reads sprint-status.yaml before doing anything else
Skips story 1.1 which is already done
Story 1.2 with ready-for-dev status starts at Step 3 (Develop), not Step 1
Uses Agent tool with team_name parameter, not a disposable sub-agent
